commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e84fc1780d2401b6c063cffce05486df22472950)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional output-file context to memory-wiki link formatting, threads it through directory indexes and related blocks, and updates native-mode link expectations.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests 0. Total +24 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: compile a native memory-wiki vault with generated subdirectory pages and the current formatter emits root-relative targets. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is a contributor-visible PR repair plus real behavior proof, so this should stay in normal review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): generate correct relative links in subdirectory files
target.body:
ormatWikiLink used page.relativePath (root-relative) as the link target regardless of the output file location. In subdirectory index.md files (e.g. concepts/index.md), this produced broken links like [Foo](concepts/foo.md) instead of [Foo](foo.md). In page-level Related blocks, links like [Foo](concepts/foo.md) from entities/bar.md were not prefixed with ../.

## Changes
- Add optional  romPath parameter to  ormatWikiLink in markdown.ts and compute correct relative links via path.posix.relative when the output file is in a subdirectory
- Thread  romPath through enderSectionList, enderWikiPageLinks,  uildDirectoryIndexBody, and  uildRelatedBlockBody in compile.ts 
- Update test expectations to match correct relative link behavior

## Verification
- compile.test.ts: 11/11 ?
- apply.test.ts: 4/4 ?
- Lint clean

Closes #89424
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
